annotations.label: Student's t distribution

anchor.snippet: is a continuous probability distribution that generalizes the standard normal distribution

annotations.note: No Student's t-distribution declaration is present in Mathlib (grep for StudentT/tDistribution under Mathlib/Probability/Distributions returned nothing).

annotations.label: Cauchy and normal limiting cases

anchor.snippet: becomes the standard Cauchy distribution , which has very

annotations.note: Cauchy and Gaussian endpoint distributions are defined, but the limiting relationship to the (undefined) t-distribution is not formalized.

annotations.label: Location-scale form generalizes normal / Bayesian compound

anchor.snippet: it generalizes the normal distribution and also arises in the Bayesian analysis of data from a normal family as a compound distribution

annotations.note: Mathlib has no location-scale t-distribution or normal/inverse-gamma compound result.

annotations.label: Probability density function

anchor.snippet: Student's t distribution has the probability density function (PDF) given by

annotations.note: No t-distribution PDF appears in Mathlib (only Cauchy/Gaussian/Beta/Gamma PDFs).

annotations.label: PDF is symmetric and bell-shaped

anchor.snippet: The probability density function is symmetric , and its overall shape resembles the bell shape

annotations.note: No t-distribution PDF (or its symmetry) is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: t distribution approaches normal as df grows

anchor.snippet: As the number of degrees of freedom grows, the t distribution approaches the normal distribution with mean 0 and variance 1.

annotations.note: Convergence of t to normal is not formalized; the t-distribution itself is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cumulative distribution function

anchor.snippet: The cumulative distribution function (CDF) can be written in terms of I , the regularized incomplete beta function

annotations.note: Mathlib has the complete Beta integral but no regularized incomplete beta function nor a t-distribution CDF.

annotations.label: Alternative CDF formula via hypergeometric function

anchor.snippet: An alternative formula, valid for

annotations.note: An ordinary hypergeometric function exists in Mathlib but no t-distribution CDF formula in terms of it.

annotations.label: Raw moments of the t distribution

anchor.snippet: the raw moments of the t distribution are

annotations.note: Neither the t-distribution nor its raw moments are defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Moments of order ν or higher do not exist

anchor.snippet: or higher do not exist.

annotations.note: No statement about non-existence of high-order t-distribution moments is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplified even-order moments

anchor.snippet: k even, may be simplified using the properties of the gamma function to

annotations.note: Real.Gamma is formalized but no even-order t-distribution moment simplification exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis

anchor.snippet: The skewness is 0 if

annotations.note: No mean/variance/skewness/kurtosis of the t-distribution exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: t distribution as distribution of test statistic T

anchor.section: As the distribution of a test statistic
anchor.snippet: can be defined as the distribution of the random variable T with

annotations.note: The ratio-of-normal-to-chi construction is absent; Mathlib lacks the chi-squared distribution.

annotations.label: Noncentral t-distribution

anchor.section: As the distribution of a test statistic
anchor.snippet: This random variable has a noncentral t -distribution with noncentrality parameter μ .

annotations.note: No noncentral t-distribution is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scaled sample variance is chi-squared (Cochran)

anchor.section: As the distribution of a test statistic
anchor.snippet: degrees of freedom (by Cochran's theorem ).

annotations.note: Neither the chi-squared distribution nor Cochran's theorem is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standardized sample mean is standard normal

anchor.section: As the distribution of a test statistic
anchor.snippet: is normally distributed with mean 0 and variance 1, since the sample mean

annotations.note: Mathlib proves sums and scalar multiples of Gaussian variables are Gaussian (HasGaussianLaw.sum/smul), but the standardized sample mean as exactly N(0,1) is not assembled.

annotations.label: Pivotal quantity has Student's t-distribution

anchor.section: As the distribution of a test statistic
anchor.snippet: which differs from Z in that the exact standard deviation σ is replaced by the sample standard error s , has a Student's t -distribution as defined above.

annotations.note: No result that the studentized pivot follows a t-distribution exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: T depends on ν but not μ or σ

anchor.section: As the distribution of a test statistic
anchor.snippet: but not μ or σ ; the lack of dependence on μ and σ is what makes the t -distribution important

annotations.note: No pivotal-quantity parameter-independence result for the t-statistic is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-sample t statistic is Student's t distributed

anchor.snippet: and is distributed according to a Student's t distribution with

annotations.note: The sampling distribution of the one-sample t-statistic is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: t statistic is a pivotal quantity

anchor.snippet: Thus for inference purposes the t statistic is a useful

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of pivotal quantity or the t-statistic.

annotations.label: MLE-based statistic is location-scale t distributed

anchor.snippet: This is distributed according to the location-scale t distribution:

annotations.note: No location-scale t-distribution or MLE-based statistic distribution result is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compound of normal with inverse gamma over variance

anchor.section: Compound distribution of normal with inverse gamma distribution
anchor.snippet: The location-scale t distribution results from compounding a Gaussian distribution (normal distribution) with mean

annotations.note: Neither the inverse-gamma distribution nor the normal/inverse-gamma compounding result is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalent compound with scaled-inverse-chi-squared

anchor.section: Compound distribution of normal with inverse gamma distribution
anchor.snippet: Equivalently, this distribution results from compounding a Gaussian distribution with a scaled-inverse-chi-squared distribution with parameters

annotations.note: Mathlib has no scaled-inverse-chi-squared distribution or this compounding equivalence.

annotations.label: t distribution as maximum entropy distribution

anchor.snippet: Student's t distribution is the maximum entropy probability distribution for a random variate X having a certain value of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no maximum-entropy characterization for the t-distribution.

annotations.label: Integral A(t|ν) of the density

anchor.section: Integral of Student's probability density function and p -value
anchor.snippet: is the integral of Student's probability density function, f ( t ) between -t and t , for t ≥ 0 .

annotations.note: The integral A(t|ν) of the t-density is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noncentral t distribution

anchor.snippet: The noncentral t distribution generalizes the t distribution to include a noncentrality parameter.

annotations.note: No noncentral t-distribution is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noncentral t-distribution is asymmetric

anchor.snippet: Unlike the nonstandardized t distributions, the noncentral distributions are not symmetric

annotations.note: The noncentral t-distribution is not in Mathlib, so its asymmetry is also not formalized.

annotations.label: Discrete Student's t distribution

anchor.snippet: The discrete Student's t distribution is defined by its probability mass function at r being proportional to:

annotations.note: No discrete Student's t-distribution is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sampling via ratio of normal and chi-squared root

anchor.snippet: One can generate Student A ( t | ν ) samples by taking the ratio of variables from the normal distribution and the square-root of the χ ² distribution .

annotations.note: The chi-squared distribution and this ratio-sampling characterization are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: t distribution as a ratio distribution

anchor.snippet: t distribution is an instance of ratio distributions .

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general ratio-distribution theory nor the t-distribution.

annotations.label: Square of t_n is F(1,n)

anchor.snippet: The square of a random variable distributed t n is distributed as Snedecor's F distribution F 1, n .

annotations.note: The F-distribution is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Location-scale t distribution

anchor.snippet: Student's t distribution generalizes to the three parameter location-scale t distribution

annotations.note: The three-parameter location-scale t-distribution is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Density of the location-scale t distribution

anchor.snippet: The location-scale t distribution has a density defined by:

annotations.note: No location-scale t-distribution density exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Location-scale t normal limit

anchor.snippet: is normally distributed

annotations.note: The normal limit of the location-scale t-distribution is not formalized.

annotations.label: Location-scale t with ν=1 is Cauchy

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to the Cauchy distribution

annotations.note: Cauchy is formalized but its identification with the ν=1 location-scale t-distribution is not (t-distribution undefined).

annotations.label: Location-scale t reduces to standard t

anchor.snippet: reduces to the Student's t distribution

annotations.note: Neither the location-scale nor the standard t-distribution is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spearman's rho approximated by t distribution

anchor.snippet: is well approximated by the t distribution for sample sizes above about 20.

annotations.note: Neither Spearman's rho statistic nor its t-approximation is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Confidence interval for the mean

anchor.snippet: is a 90% confidence interval for μ.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no confidence-interval machinery.

annotations.label: One-sided upper confidence limit

anchor.snippet: If the data are normally distributed, the one-sided (1 − α ) upper confidence limit (UCL) of the mean, can be calculated using the following equation:

annotations.note: No one-sided confidence-limit construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prediction interval from t distribution

anchor.snippet: The t distribution can be used to construct a prediction interval for an unobserved sample from a normal distribution with unknown mean and variance.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no prediction-interval construction.

annotations.label: Marginal of normal with inverse-gamma prior is t

anchor.snippet: the resulting marginal distribution of the variable will follow a Student's t distribution.

annotations.note: Bayesian conjugate-prior marginalization yielding a t-distribution is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Student's t process

anchor.snippet: A Student's t process is constructed from the Student t distributions like a Gaussian process is constructed from the Gaussian distributions .

annotations.note: The analogous IsGaussianProcess is formalized, but no Student's t process exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Confidence interval computation example

anchor.snippet: Let's say we have a sample with size 11, sample mean 10, and sample variance 2.

annotations.note: This worked confidence-interval example is not formalized in Mathlib.
